summary: Completed frontend-only regression after Slack-style theme overhaul. All requested routes loaded, core interactions remained usable, recurring template actions worked, and no desktop horizontal overflow was detected. No APIs were MOCKED.

issues: Some tiny muted text appears low-contrast and very small against light backgrounds, reducing readability for secondary metadata.
Floating 'Made with Emergent' badge can overlap bottom-right content area on long screens.

action_items: Improve contrast/size for tiny metadata text styles (e.g., .tiny + .muted combinations).
Consider offset/avoidance behavior for bottom-right floating badge to prevent potential overlap with actionable content.
critical_code_review_comments: App.css is very large (>1200 lines) and should be split by layout/components/screens for maintainability.
Large global style changes increase regression risk; add a lightweight route-level visual regression check in CI for main screens.
seed_data_creation: No file-based seed data created; existing local demo data used.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Validated requested routes: dashboard, invoices, recurring, projects, clients, settings, invoice detail, editor, project detail. Verified recurring template list and actions (toggle/edit) and milestone send-invoice navigation. No route breakage reproduced from earlier iteration.
rca of the issue: No functional integration failure reproduced in this run. Remaining findings are visual/readability polish risks after global CSS token/theme replacement.
